title: CASE LAW QUICK REFERENCE CARDS
subtitle: Key Authorities for Court — Organized by Legal Theory

cases.holding: Parents have fundamental liberty interest in care and custody of children
cases.use: ANY hearing where parenting time is at issue

cases.holding: Clear and convincing standard required before terminating parental rights
cases.use: Challenge any order that effectively terminates contact

cases.holding: Unwed fathers have protected liberty interest in parental relationship
Due process requires recusal when probability of bias is too high

cases.case: Crampton v Dept of State, 395 Mich 347 (1975)
cases.holding: Objective test for judicial bias — appearance of impropriety sufficient
Pattern of conduct establishes bias — no need for single smoking gun
Private parties who conspire with judge lose state-action immunity
cases.use: Include Emily, Berry, Barnes as co-defendants

cases.holding: §1983 claims for custody interference are NOT barred by domestic relations exception
1st Amendment retaliation: protected activity + adverse action + causal connection
cases.use: McNeill retaliated for Andrew filing motions

cases.holding: Courts have inherent power to vacate judgments obtained by fraud
cases.use: Vacate ALL orders based on Emily's fraud

cases.holding: Inherent power to sanction fraud includes full costs and fees

cases.case: Aoude v Mobil Oil, 892 F.2d 1115 (1st Cir 1989)
cases.holding: Party who obtains judgment through perjury forfeits all claims
cases.use: Emily's perjury voids her custody position

cases.holding: Parenting time SHALL be granted — presumption in favor
Suspension ONLY with clear and convincing evidence of danger
cases.use: Challenge suspension — no such finding made

cases.holding: Court SHALL provide make-up time for wrongfully denied visits
Relief from judgment for fraud — within 1 year

cases.use: Orders entered without jurisdiction/due process are void ab initio

cases.holding: Independent action for fraud on court — NO time bar
cases.use: Attack foundational fraud at any time
